conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex, and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand your relationship and living situation with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for him? (This means you're involved in decisions about his upbringing and you contribute to his care, such as food, clothing, or pocket money.)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live with Alex and I'm responsible for him. I take care of all his needs - food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to check a few more details:

**Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. I need to ask about your immigration status:

**Do you have the right to live in the UK? (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits?)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen. I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- Living with someone else (not you) for more than part of the time?
- In hospital or residential care?
- In detention (such as a young offender institution)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Alex lives with me full-time at home. He's not in hospital or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the necessary criteria:

✓ You are responsible for Alex - you live with him and take care of his needs
✓ Alex is under 16 years old (he's 8)
✓ You both live in the UK
✓ You have the right to live in the UK as a UK citizen
✓ Alex lives with you full-time
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. You'll need to complete a claim form, and you may need to provide Alex's birth certificate as part of the application process.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the required criteria: (1) They are responsible for Alex - they live with him and contribute to his care including food, clothing, and school needs; (2) Alex is under 16 years old (he is 8); (3) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (Manchester); (4) The claimant has the right to live in the UK as they are a UK citizen; (5) Alex lives with the claimant full-time and is not in hospital, care, or detention; (6) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex, live with him full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
